sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: West Ham United are in poor form, with no wins in their last 4 matches and only 1 win in their last 5, including a heavy 0-3 loss to Tottenham. Brentford, on the other hand, have shown more resilience with a recent 3-1 win over Manchester United, though they’ve struggled for consistency, winning only 2 of their last 5 games. West Ham's defensive frailties (conceding 16 goals this season) contrast with Brentford's ability to score late, as seen in their goal distribution.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Brentford have the edge, winning 1-0 at West Ham earlier this year and drawing 1-1 in the reverse fixture. Brentford have scored first in 4 of their last 5 meetings, putting West Ham on the back foot early. Historically, matches between these sides tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 clashes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: West Ham sit 19th in the league with just 4 points, struggling offensively (6 goals scored, 18th rank) and defensively (16 conceded, 19th rank). Brentford, positioned 16th with 7 points, have a slightly better attack (9 goals scored) but also leak goals (12 conceded). West Ham’s higher corner average (5.57 per game) and Brentford’s tendency for cards (often over 4.5 per game) could shape match dynamics.

sections.subtitle: Key Match Insights
sections.text: West Ham’s inability to keep clean sheets (none in last 4 games) and tendency to concede first (5 of 6 matches) could be exploited by Brentford, who often strike early or late. Brentford’s away form remains inconsistent, but their pressing and counter-attacking style might trouble West Ham’s shaky defense. Sofascore’s prediction of a 42% chance for an away win aligns with current trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Brentford lead West Ham United 1-0 at the London Stadium. The visitors struck late in the first half through Igor Thiago, capitalizing on their dominant attacking play. West Ham struggled to create meaningful chances, while Brentford looked far more threatening in the final third. The home side will need a significant turnaround in the second half to get back into this game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Brentford have been the superior side in almost every department during the first half. They controlled possession and showed greater intent with frequent entries into the final third and penalty area. Their ability to create big chances stood out, even if they missed a few opportunities before scoring. West Ham, on the other hand, lacked creativity and sharpness in attack, managing only a handful of attempts on goal. Defensively, the Hammers were under constant pressure, losing most duels and aerial battles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at half-time
halftimeSections.text: For Brentford, Igor Thiago has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat up front. Michael Kayode also impressed with his contributions in build-up play, while players like Dango Ouattara and Mikkel Damsgaard showed flair in creating chances. On the West Ham side, Jarrod Bowen was the most active in attack, though his efforts yielded little reward. Defenders like Max Kilman and Jean-Clair Todibo worked hard to contain Brentford’s relentless pressure but struggled to organize effectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with West Ham showing some early initiative, pushing forward in the opening minutes with a few attacking spells. However, momentum quickly shifted to Brentford, who took control around the midway point of the half and maintained sustained pressure for long periods. Their attacking waves grew more intense as the half progressed, culminating in the goal just before the break. West Ham had brief moments of resurgence late in the half, but they lacked the consistency to truly challenge Brentford’s dominance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: West Ham will likely need to adopt a more aggressive approach in the second half to overturn the deficit. Manager David Moyes might push for quicker transitions and utilize players like Lucas Paquetá and Crysencio Summerville to unlock Brentford’s defense. Brentford, under Thomas Frank, could look to manage the game by maintaining possession and exploiting counter-attacking opportunities. Their confidence will be high, and they’ll aim to capitalize on any defensive lapses from the hosts.
